compilersJustification: BabyBear (bbFieldMul) + SHA-256 Merkle (merkleRootSha256) are EVM/STARK proof-system primitives that are Go-only by project policy — see CLAUDE.md "EVM/STARK proof-system primitives are Go-only by project policy" and the project_go_only_crypto_modules memory. The TS/Rust/Python/Zig/Ruby tiers may carry partial ports for historical reasons but are NOT conformance targets for these families; only the Go tier is the canonical reference. Java is not exempt because of a deferred port — it is exempt because the entire family is Go-only. The non-Go frontends still parse the contract via the all-tier parser-only matrix so CompileCheck coverage is preserved.
